Hey guys,
Ideal range for ensuring chocolate crystals don't form in our holding tanks is about 120 degrees (this differs between milk/dark/paste)
I set it to 49 degrees knowing that the chocolate itself will be cooler than the water jacket setting. 
I also updated the CL for CNC to have the buffer tank at that setpoint but haven't done it to any other CL
Gustavo/Alan

Happy to discuss further and change it if needed

These covers were a custom request to replace the big bulky fiberglass ones that keep breaking because they are so big and heavy. There is no air cooling on that tunnel, so it was really just to cover the product. But if QA would rather get rid of items that will break, we can just leave the conveyor uncovered. It shouldn't affect the product or functionality of the cooling belt.

During the afternoon of this shift on the Truffles line, work is focused on item 20062-S (WIP). Generally speaking, the line is flowing smoothly and without deviations. We began with an approximate weight of 13.3; adjustments were subsequently made to bring both the pre-piece and the final product weight within the specified range. Regarding both the screening and tempering processes, there are no issues to report; it has been observed that the bottom coating of the product has improved. The flow-wrapped packages are well-sealed, with all data complete and accurate. On the Cavanna cutting machine, some pieces are emerging with their bases facing upward; however, the production team is currently working to rectify this issue. Palletizing is being carried out in accordance with specifications; regarding the inspection for damaged covers, these are being monitored periodically, with no major problems to report.
The tank was left higher than the directed amount. I will review with the team tomorrow the standard

Curt Willis, Justin Garner, Marcos Jimenez, & Daniel Bringas — I just reviewed Volker’s schedule for returning to support Kitchen 4 startup of the Twist Wrap line. Since he has completed his work with Italu, he would like to make 3x - 1,000 lb batches of Classic Vanilla Caramel tomorrow to confirm the Kitchen 4 is running correctly before he leaves.
 
The intent is to reduce risk in the event there are any flight delays or scheduling challenges when he returns for Twist Wrap startup support.
 
Curt Willis, could you please confirm if we can plan to do this tomorrow?
Jorge Carrillo,  did you and Curt Willis connect on this?
Nope we did not
Hi Team, I’m in Sollich 4 Kitchen with Volker. Will we cook 1, 2, or 3 batches (1,000 lb each) today, or will we not cook today? Thanks. Curt Willis Jorge Carrillo Justin Garner
Please cook the 3 1000lbs batches to confirm everything is running correctly.

Please cook 2 or 3 batches.

It can go on the table to confirm consistency than be scrapped
